title: Large Firewood Box
summary: A big plywood-panel firewood bin with angled 2x2 corner supports and a 1x12 open top rim, sized to hold a real stack of split wood indoors or on a porch.
description:
Four 3/4" plywood panels form the box's sides, bottom, and back — cut all four to the same 24" x 48" size from a single sheet, which is what keeps this build fast and material-efficient. The 2x2 corner supports tie the panels together, with two of them cut at a 30° angle on one end and two more cut with both ends at 30° to give the box's front a slight backward lean, so loaded wood settles against the back rather than spilling forward.

**Getting the angle direction right on the four different 2x2 pieces matters** — the plan calls out three genuinely different cuts (straight, one end angled, both ends angled and parallel), so sort and label them before assembly rather than trusting them to look the same.

The three 1x12 boards cap the open top, giving the box a finished rim to grab when loading or emptying wood. This is a big, heavy-duty build once loaded, so screw every joint solidly and add glue at the corners for real strength.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet (or precut 48 in x 24 in panels)
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 3/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet (or precut 48 in x 24 in panels)

cutList.note: One end cut at 30° off square, short point measurement.
Both ends cut at 30° off square, long point to short point, ends parallel.

steps.title: Cut and sort the corner supports
steps.body: Cut the four panels and the three different types of 2x2 corner supports, sorting and labeling each angle type.
materials: 3/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet (or precut 48 in x 24 in panels)

steps.title: Assemble the box
steps.body: Attach the corner supports to the panels to form the box sides, bottom, and back, using the angled supports to create the backward lean.

steps.title: Add the top rim
steps.body: Attach the three 1x12 boards across the open top to cap the box.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Large firewood storage box with angled corner supports
